headline: Israel Deploys Tanks Near Nabatieh as Gaza Strikes Eliminate Hamas Leaders
subheadline: Prime minister, defense minister and IDF chief of staff called for action as tanks moved 1.5 km from Nabatieh; IDF airstrikes killed two Hamas commanders in Gaza.
lede: Israel's top leadership held a high‑level call urging decisive measures to protect soldiers and civilians while reinforcing the security zone in southern Lebanon. Tanks were positioned just 1.5 km from Nabatieh in the village of Kfar Rumman, signalling a hardened stance on the Lebanese border. Simultaneously, IDF airstrikes in northern Gaza eliminated Hamas West Jabalia Battalion chief engineer Ahmad Munir Khalil Zaza and sniper commander Hussein Safadi, as well as senior Nukhba operative Sabaei Zahir Abd al‑Hamid Abu Hasna. Iran pursued diplomatic outreach in Muscat and Switzerland, while the U.S. granted a 60‑day license for Iranian‑origin oil sales.

sections.title: Ukraine Expands Strikes Into Russia, Secures Patriot Deal
sections.body: Ukrainian forces hit the "Сборка" semiconductor plant in Voronezh, damaging a key producer of Pantsir, Iskander‑K and Kh‑101 missile components. A Ukrainian drone struck a civilian bus in Gorlovka during rush hour, injuring thirteen passengers. Russia opened a criminal case accusing Ukraine of a terrorist attack on Voronezh and later hit a Ukrainian fuel depot in Zaporozhye with Geran missiles. Kyiv also signed a contract with Germany for delivery of 600 Patriot interceptor missiles.

sections.title: Israel Tightens Lebanon Front Amid Gaza Operations
sections.body: Israel's prime minister, defense minister and IDF chief of staff emphasized decisive action to protect troops and civilians while maintaining the southern Lebanon security zone. Tanks were reported operating 1.5 km from Nabatieh in the Lebanese village of Kfar Rumman. In northern Gaza, IDF airstrikes killed Hamas West Jabalia Battalion chief engineer Ahmad Munir Khalil Zaza, sniper commander Hussein Safadi, and senior Nukhba operative Sabaei Zahir Abd al‑Hamid Abu Hasna. Iranian parliament speaker Ghalibaf and foreign minister Araghchi traveled to Muscat for talks on bilateral relations and joint management of the Strait of Hormuz, as the U.S. issued a 60‑day license for Iranian‑origin oil sales.

sections.title: Intelligence Assessment
sections.body: Israel faces a multi‑front pressure point, with tank deployments near Nabatieh and intensified Gaza strikes indicating a broadened operational tempo. Ukraine's cross‑border UAV attacks on Russian industrial and civilian targets suggest an expanding strike capability, while the pending Patriot delivery could shift the aerial balance in its favor.
